q: What is the difference between a connector and a connection?
o: A connector is the definition; a connection is an authenticated instance of it
A connection is the definition; a connector is the instance
A connection is a Dataverse table
why: The connector defines operations; a connection is a specific authenticated use of it.

q: In Visual Studio, how can you expose your web API to Power Platform quickly?
o: Add Power Platform as a Connected Service
Store it in Dataverse as a row
why: The Connected Services experience in VS scaffolds a custom connector for your API.

q: Decide whether each authoring-path statement is true.

rows.s: A custom connector can be created from an OpenAPI document.
A custom connector can be created from Azure API Management.
Custom connectors can only be built by hand in the portal.

why: You can author from OpenAPI, from APIM, from VS Connected Services, or by hand.

q: Which document format is commonly imported to define a connector's operations?
why: OpenAPI (Swagger) describes the API and auto-populates connector operations.

q: After adding Power Platform as a connected service in VS, what can you generate to try it?
o: An app that consumes the API via the connector
why: The tooling can create an app from the API to preview the connector working.

q: Why run your API first before finalizing the connector from Visual Studio?
o: So the tooling can read its endpoints/definition to build the connector
why: Running the API lets the tooling discover its endpoints/OpenAPI to scaffold the connector.

q: Decide whether each VS/connected-service statement is true.

rows.s: You can preview the generated app to validate the connector.
The connected service maps your API into a custom connector.

why: Connected Services maps the API to a connector and you can preview an app to validate it.

q: Web APIs placed 'behind Azure API Management' benefit from...
o: A managed gateway (security, throttling, versioning) in front of the API
why: APIM provides a gateway offering security, rate-limiting, and versioning for backend APIs.

q: How do you turn an API managed by APIM into a Power Platform connector?
o: Export the API from API Management to a custom connector
why: API Management can export an API definition directly as a custom connector.

q: After exporting from APIM, what must a maker do to actually call the connector?
why: A connection authenticates the connector so apps/flows can invoke its operations.

q: Decide whether each APIM statement is true.

rows.s: APIM can front multiple backend APIs behind one gateway.
Exporting from APIM speeds up creating a custom connector.

why: APIM fronts backends and its export accelerates connector creation.

q: Which end-to-end path is valid for surfacing a custom REST API in a flow?
o: Define via OpenAPI/APIM/VS -> custom connector -> connection -> use in flow
Add it as a Dataverse column
Put the URL in the app title
why: Author the connector (OpenAPI/APIM/VS), create a connection, then use it in apps/flows.
